description: The most dramatically beautiful city in the world — Table Mountain rising above the Atlantic, penguin colonies on the beach, vineyards 30 minutes from the CBD, and a food and design scene punching well above its weight.

neighborhoodRecs: De Waterkant / Green Point — Gay-friendly, walkable to V&A Waterfront, restaurants, close to beaches, safest area for visitors.
Atlantic Seaboard (Sea Point, Clifton, Camps Bay) — Stunning ocean-facing strip, upscale restaurants, beach bars, sunset views.
Gardens / City Bowl — Close to Table Mountain, bohemian cafes, excellent restaurants, great base for first visit.
Woodstock / Salt River — Emerging creative neighbourhood, art galleries, craft beer, trendy restaurants — excellent for longer stays.
areasToAvoid: Central CBD at night — empties quickly, isolated streets can be unsafe. Take Uber everywhere after dark.
Khayelitsha and Mitchells Plain townships without a responsible guided tour — not places for independent wandering.
Walking alone on isolated mountain paths — always hike with a group or through the cable car in busy periods.
scamWarnings: Car guards: informal parking attendants will 'watch your car' and expect R5–20 when you return. Paying is safer than not.
Distraction pickpockets on the V&A Waterfront — tourist areas attract coordinated teams. Keep bags in front.
Fake injury scams near the taxi rank — someone 'falls' near you and demands compensation for injury. Walk away.
Wildlife sanctuary tourism: some leopard petting and lion cub encounters involve animals taken from wild populations. Research operators.

airportTransfer.cheapest: MyCiTi Bus from CPT airport to central Cape Town / Civic Centre — R100, takes 50–70 min. Runs every 20–30 min.
airportTransfer.fastest: Uber from CPT — R200–300 ($11–17) to CBD/Waterfront, takes 20–30 min.
airportTransfer.recommended: Uber for most travellers — straightforward, safe, air-conditioned. MyCiTi for budget solo travellers.
airportTransfer.taxiEstimate: Registered metered taxi R300–450 to CBD. Avoid informal minibus taxis from the airport — they're for locals who know the routes.

tippingEtiquette: 10–15% at restaurants is standard. Service charge is rarely included — tip in cash separately.
Car guards: R5–10 when you return to your car. They are an informal but important part of neighbourhood safety.
Hotel staff: R20–50 per day for housekeeping is generous and appreciated.
South Africa has extreme inequality — your tip is significant. Be generous where you can.
Never walk alone at night, even in 'safe' areas — always Uber. This is not optional in Cape Town.

dailyCostBreakdown.accommodation: Hostel dorm R200–400 | Guesthouse R600–1,200 | Boutique hotel R1,500–3,500 | Villa R5,000+
dailyCostBreakdown.meals: Street bobotie R40–60 | Café lunch R120–200 | Restaurant dinner R250–500 | Fine dining R700+
dailyCostBreakdown.transport: Uber within city R50–150 | MyCiTi bus R20–100 | Table Mountain cable car R420 return
dailyCostBreakdown.activities: Table Mountain cable car R420 | Boulders Beach penguins R220 | Cape Point R380 | Robben Island R650

packingTips: Layers for summer — the Cape Doctor wind (SE summer wind) is intense and can cool temperatures by 10°C instantly.
Sunscreen SPF 50+ — the Southern hemisphere sun is fierce and the ozone layer is thinner.
Good walking shoes for Table Mountain — Platteklip Gorge hike requires proper footwear.
Fleece for winter (Jun–Aug) — temperatures drop to 8°C and the rain is frequent and heavy.
premium.bookingStrategy: December–February is peak season (school holidays + summer) — book 3–4 months ahead and expect prices 50% higher. November and March offer near-peak weather with substantially lower prices and fewer crowds. Table Mountain cable car books up — buy online.
localTips: Table Mountain sunrise hike via Platteklip Gorge takes 2.5 hours and you'll have it nearly to yourself before 7am.
Saturday Oranjezicht City Farm Market in De Waterkant: the best artisan food market in the city — arrive hungry.
Boulders Beach penguins (Boulder's Colony near Simon's Town) is one of the world's great wildlife experiences — accessible by MyCiTi train.
The Cape Winelands (Stellenbosch, Franschhoek) are 30–45 min drive — rent a car or book a wine tour for the most spectacular vineyard dining.
Sunset from Signal Hill or Lion's Head is completely free and one of the best views in Africa.
premium.bestTimeToVisit: November or March: 21–24°C, manageable crowds, hotels at 60% of peak price, clear skies for Table Mountain views.
dayTrips: Cape Peninsula — Chapman's Peak Drive, Cape Point, Boulders Beach penguins, circular route in a hire car, full day.
Stellenbosch & Franschhoek — 40 min drive, world-class wine estates, Huguenot architecture, Cape Dutch farmsteads.
Whale Watching at Hermanus — 90 min east (July–November), southern right whales breach within metres of the cliffs.
visaInfo: Citizens of the US, UK, EU countries, Canada, and Australia: visa-free entry for 90 days as tourists — a valid passport is all that is required
South Africa requires your passport to have at least 30 days validity beyond your planned departure date and at least one blank page for the entry stamp
Children travelling with parents should carry an unabridged birth certificate (especially if only one parent is present) — this is actively enforced at South African borders
Yellow fever vaccination certificate required if arriving from a country with risk of yellow fever transmission (most of sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South America)
Apply for an extension at the Department of Home Affairs in Cape Town if you wish to stay beyond 90 days — not easily granted for tourists
currencyTips: Currency: South African Rand (ZAR / R). Notes come in R10, R20, R50, R100, R200 denominations
Cards accepted widely in Cape Town restaurants, shops, and hotels; contactless payment works at most modern venues in the city; cash essential in townships and markets
Best ATMs: Standard Bank and ABSA ATMs are the most reliable for foreign cards; avoid standalone ATMs in shopping centres (higher fees and card skimming risk)
Tipping is important in South Africa — built into the culture; 10–15% at restaurants, R20–50 to parking attendants, R50–100 to safari guides
Beware of card skimming at petrol stations and older standalone ATMs — always use bank-branch ATMs and cover the keypad when entering your PIN
premium.simCard: Vodacom and MTN are the two dominant carriers in Cape Town — both have excellent 4G coverage in the city, the Cape Peninsula, and the Garden Route. A prepaid tourist SIM with 5–10 GB data costs R50–150 at the airport, supermarkets (Pick n Pay, Checkers), or phone shops. eSIM via Airalo is a good alternative for shorter stays.

emergencyNumbers.emergency: 112 (works from all mobile phones, including without a SIM)
